issues.title: Precise-minimalism pass over remaining template artifacts
issues.description: PHILOSOPHY.md and TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md seeds were trimmed to instruction-only. Sweep PROCESS.md, the CONTEXT.md seed, the CLAUDE.md stub, and the agent defs for explanatory prose that is not instruction.
issues.fix: Precise-minimalism sweep: 5 explanatory clauses cut (PROCESS.md x3, scribe.md x1, nothing-new-under-the-sun.md x1); CONTEXT.md seed and remaining agent defs verified instruction-only; no CLAUDE.md stub exists (pointer text is code, out of scope). alignment PASS; root mirrored.

issues.title: Decide whether the .excn work-tracking set stays gitignored (issues should be visible in-repo)
issues.description: The Scaffolder stamps .excn/.gitignore (src/template/.excn/gitignore, restored at stamp time) ignoring sprints/, issues/, prds/, retros/, *_progress.json. A gitignored backlog can't be seen, reviewed, or shared from the repo. Decide (HITL, Brooks): version-control the full set, keep status quo, or a middle path tracking issues/ (maybe prds/, sprints/) while keeping *_progress.json and retros/ ignored. Changes the Scaffolder's invariant layout and brushes the JSON-for-progress Principle, so record the decision as an ADR; a Principle-level change also routes through the alignment gate. Src-First: src/template/.excn/gitignore changes first, root Instance mirrors. Fix while here: root .excn/.gitignore also ignores drafts/, which the shipped template does not.
acceptance_criteria: An ADR records the decision with the trade-off (visible/reviewable backlog vs. ephemeral-state noise)
src/template/.excn/gitignore implements the decision and the root .excn/.gitignore mirrors it
Root-vs-template drift (drafts/) is resolved
package-qa passes on the stamp contract
issues.fix: ADR-0005; template gitignore = *_progress.json only, root mirrors, drafts/ removed; package-qa PASS.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Owner: builder; ADR + alignment gate for the Principle brush.

issues.title: Spike/grill: hook-invoked lightweight reviewers; offload secretary work to smaller models
issues.description: Today the Team Lead manually spawns the Adherence/adversarial agents, and they run at full weight even for mechanical checks. Explore scaffolding Claude Code hooks into the Template so reviewers auto-invoke at the right workflow moments, and route routine secretary work (scribe's structured-JSON updates, progress-record maintenance) to a smaller model. Grounding: .excn/research/teammate-hook-triggers.md §3.1/§6 (Stop block+reason as file-update reminder; PostToolUse additionalContext for status-based instruction injection), §0 (actor identity in the hook payload). Caveat §3.1: additionalContext is model-judged — injected guidance must read as legitimate ops instruction. Exploratory: first step is a spike/grill deciding which reviewers are safe to auto-fire and which secretary tasks are mechanical enough for a smaller model. The Template ships no hook wiring today; this spike also designs the shared hook-scaffolding plumbing EXEC-044/EXEC-045 ride on. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: A grill→PRD (or recorded spike outcome) names which reviewers auto-fire, at which hook events, and which secretary tasks move to a smaller model
The shared Template hook-scaffolding plumbing for EXEC-044/EXEC-045 is designed
issues.fix: Sprint-4 grill resolved the design; PRD-007 names the remind-only reviewers/hook events and the clerk offload; ADR-0006 records the plumbing EXEC-044/EXEC-045 ride on (EXEC-046).
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Grill-first per the Lifecycle — this slice produces a PRD/spike record, not code.

issues.title: Scaffold a hook that nudges agents to follow through on messages they send teammates
issues.description: When a Teammate messages another, a hook senses it and reminds the sender to do the related follow-through (update a record, file an issue, pass a gate) driven off message content. Grounding: teammate-hook-triggers.md §4.3 (inbox store ~/.claude/teams/<team>/inboxes/<agent>.json as sense signal and async nudge channel), §3 (PostToolUse → additionalContext as the in-session nudge path), §0 (sender identity). Resolved at the sprint-4 grill: trigger on SENT messages, in the sender's session (ADR-0006). Rides the EXEC-046 plumbing; ships disabled by default. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: Sending a teammate message triggers a follow-through nudge in the sender's session via additionalContext
The nudge names the follow-through implied by the message (record update, issue, gate)
Ships disabled by default; enabling via the toggle config activates it
No recipient-side watching
issues.fix: message-nudge.js implemented (sent-side additionalContext follow-through nudge, rules in nudge-rules.js, ships off); payload field names probed not live-confirmed — confirm against a live SendMessage payload before enabling. Gates PASS.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Exploratory; acceptance criteria set by the EXEC-043 grill/PRD.

issues.title: Hook-based teammate load reporting, surfaced in the status-page viewer
issues.description: A hook logs per-teammate activity so we can see which Teammates bear the most load, surfaced in the status-page viewer. Grounding: teammate-hook-triggers.md §0 (every tool event carries agent_type/agent_id; the §0 probe is a per-tool JSONL append — the telemetry mechanism), §1 (PostToolUse/PostToolBatch as the 'agent did X' signal), §2.3 (identity disambiguation). Spans two owners: hook wiring → builder (Template), reporting UI → viewer. Resolved at the sprint-4 grill: the record is hook-appended .excn/load_progress.json (the *_progress.json ignore class, ADR-0005), viewer-fetchable. Rides the EXEC-046 plumbing; ships disabled by default. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: The hook appends per-tool-event load records to .excn/load_progress.json with identity from agent_type/agent_id
load_progress.json has a schema in .excn/schemas/ and stays in the *_progress.json ignore class
The status-page viewer renders a per-Teammate load lane from it
Ships disabled by default; enabling via the toggle config activates it
issues.fix: Hook side: load-report.js + load-progress.schema.json (rolling 5000-record window, ships off). Viewer side: per-Teammate load panel in viewer/ (absent-file off-state, broken-file named error, agent_type/agent_id aggregation). Gates PASS (viewer lane after one revise cycle: ONE_MINUTE_MS).
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Exploratory; acceptance criteria set by the EXEC-043 grill/PRD. Hook side and viewer lane both shipped + gated 2026-06-10.

issues.title: Hook plumbing: .excn toggle config + schema + stamped settings wiring
issues.description: The Scaffolder stamps the hook scaffolding's shared plumbing: a schema-validated toggle config in .excn (one enable flag per hook feature; schema added to the Instance schemas) and the project settings wiring that routes hook events to per-feature scripts. A missing or malformed config fails safe to no-op. Src-First; per ADR-0006.
acceptance_criteria: A freshly stamped Instance carries the toggle config, its schema, and the settings wiring
Each feature script no-ops when its toggle is off, when the config is missing, or when it is malformed
package-qa passes on the stamp contract
Root Instance mirrors src/ (Src-First)
issues.fix: Stamped hooks.config.json + hooks-config.schema.json + .claude/settings.json wiring + 5 fail-safe hook scripts; package-qa PASS, code-standards PASS.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Record restored 2026-06-10 after an id collision with a parallel-session filing (now EXEC-054).

issues.title: Gate-reminder hook: path-watch reminder + stop-block on missing gate verdict (default on)
issues.description: The first hook feature on the EXEC-046 plumbing, shipped enabled by default: a post-tool watch on gate-relevant paths injects a gate-due reminder via additionalContext, and a Stop-time block catches an agent idling with gated edits but no gate verdict recorded. Remind-only — it never spawns a gate (ADR-0006).
acceptance_criteria: Editing a gated path produces the gate-due reminder in the editing agent's context
An agent idling with gated edits and no gate verdict is stop-blocked once (loop-guarded via stop_hook_active)
The feature ships enabled by default and disabling it via the toggle config silences both behaviors
No hook path spawns an agent
issues.fix: gate-watch.js: PostToolUse path-watch reminder + Stop block-once with stop_hook_active guard + verdict sensing; ships enabled; code-standards PASS.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Record restored 2026-06-10 after an id collision with a parallel-session filing (now EXEC-055).

issues.title: clerk Invoked Agent (small model) + scribe delegation of mechanical record moves
issues.description: Add a clerk Invoked Agent pinned to a small model for the mechanical, schema-validated record moves: partition moves at sprint boundaries, status flips, verdict appends. scribe's definition delegates those steps to clerk and keeps judgment work; scribe remains owner of the work-tracking artifacts. Chartered definition change (ADR-0004 path). Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: clerk agent definition exists in the Template and the root Instance, pinned to a small model
scribe's definition routes mechanical moves to clerk and retains judgment work
alignment PASS on both definition changes; Team Lead approval recorded
A representative partition move executed by clerk validates against the issue schema
issues.fix: clerk.md (haiku-pinned) + scribe delegation bullet; alignment PASS, Team Lead approved.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Record restored 2026-06-10 after an id collision with a parallel-session filing (now EXEC-056). Clerk's first live delegation deferred to next session (definition not loadable mid-session); the demo criterion rides the next sprint-boundary move.

issues.title: Update path: stamp records framework version; update command re-stamps invariants and reports drift
issues.description: Stamping records the framework version in the Instance. An update command (the Scaffolder's second verb) re-stamps only the invariant layout at the installed package's version: variant files (the grill's project-specific outputs) and work-tracking state are never touched; invariant files that drifted locally from their stamped form are reported, not overwritten. Demoable: stamp a scratch Instance at the previous version, run update, observe invariants refreshed, variants intact, drift reported.
acceptance_criteria: A stamped Instance records the framework version that stamped it
The update command refreshes invariant files to the installed version and never modifies variant files or work-tracking state
Locally drifted invariant files are reported and left in place
package-qa passes on the stamp contract
issues.fix: framework-version.json marker on init + update verb (re-stamps invariants, drift persists via template-hash anchor, restores missing); stamp-policy.js data module; code-standards PASS, package-qa PASS.

issues.title: Health check: per-feature hook status (enabled/firing/stale/broken) + outdated via the version stamp
issues.description: A user-runnable health check reporting per-feature status rather than pass/fail: wiring present, toggle config parses against its schema, enabled hooks show recent firing evidence (heartbeat in the progress records), and the Instance's recorded stamp version matches the installed package (outdated detection, via EXEC-049's version stamp). The check is how hook decay surfaces — hooks themselves fail safe to no-op (PRD-007 testing decisions).
acceptance_criteria: One command reports per-feature status: enabled, firing, stale, or broken
An out-of-date Instance is reported as outdated with the recorded vs installed versions
A deliberately broken config and a disarmed hook are each detected and named
Runnable by an end user on any Instance
issues.fix: doctor verb: per-feature enabled/disabled/firing/stale/broken + config validation + outdated via version marker; health-policy.js data module; code-standards PASS (after one revise cycle: named time constants), package-qa PASS.

issues.title: nothing-new-under-the-sun: stamped prior-art research Invoked Agent for scoping
issues.description: Author a lightweight Invoked Agent, stamped by default in the Template, that runs during scoping (between PRD and issue breakdown). Invocation contract: a PRD id/path plus pointers to the relevant parts of the codebase. Behavior: web-search for prior art on the problem the PRD solves — YouTube videos, Stack Overflow threads, Medium/blog articles, reference implementations — and return a digest of existing approaches and common gotchas in the candidate implementation paths, each with its source. It informs scoping only: it does not decide, edit files, or gate work. Authored from the framework's agent-def shape; stamped as invariant layout (Scaffolder copies it; package-qa contract). Src-First; root Instance mirrors so this team can use it immediately.
acceptance_criteria: The agent definition ships in the Template and is stamped into a fresh Instance
Invoked with a PRD and codebase pointers, it returns a sourced digest of prior art and implementation gotchas
It performs no file edits and issues no verdicts
alignment PASS on the definition; package-qa passes on the stamp contract
Demonstrated once against PRD-007 itself on this Instance
issues.fix: Definition stamped + alignment PASS + TL approved; acceptance demo run against PRD-007, digest persisted to .excn/research/prior-art-prd-007.md; stamp contract package-qa PASS.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks, added scope at execution kickoff). Chartered definition addition — alignment-gated, Team-Lead-approved (ADR-0004 path).
